name: QR Code Generation (Core Image)
promptInjection:
CAPABILITY: QR Code Generation (Core Image CIQRCodeGenerator)

Use Core Image's CIQRCodeGenerator to create QR code images from a string. No camera or scanning — this is for generating/displaying QR codes (e.g. tickets, passes, links).

APPROACH:
- Use CIFilter(name: "CIQRCodeGenerator") — there is no type-safe CIFilterBuiltins variant for QR, so the string-based API is correct.
- Set input: setValue(Data(string.utf8), forKey: "inputMessage"). Optional: setValue("H", forKey: "inputCorrectionLevel") for higher error correction (L, M, Q, H).
- The filter output is a tiny CIImage (e.g. ~27×27). Scale it for display: use outputImage.transformed(by: CGAffineTransform(scaleX: scale, y: scale)) where scale = desiredSize / outputImage.extent.width, then render the scaled CIImage with CIContext.createCGImage(from:).
- Create CIContext once and reuse (expensive); do not create a new context per generation.
- Convert to SwiftUI: UIImage(cgImage:) then Image(uiImage:). Return SwiftUI Image or an optional placeholder when input is empty or generation fails.

SWIFTUI:
- Expose a helper that takes a String (and optional size) and returns Image. Use in views as Image or in a dedicated QR display view. Handle empty string (show placeholder text or gray rect).

ANTI-PATTERNS:
- Do NOT use CIQRCodeGenerator for scanning/reading — that uses AVCaptureMetadataOutput or Vision (see barcode-scanning skill).
- Do NOT create a new CIContext for every QR generation; reuse one.
- Do NOT forget to scale the filter output; unscaled QR is too small to scan.
- Do NOT assume inputMessage is a URL only — any UTF-8 string is valid.
antiPatterns: Using CIQRCodeGenerator for scanning/reading (use AVCaptureMetadataOutput or Vision instead)
Creating a new CIContext for every QR generation (reuse one)
Displaying the raw filter output without scaling (too small to scan)
Assuming only URLs can be encoded (any UTF-8 string is valid)
